zh.lifeSummary: 當Blanche de la Roche於1255年出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 斯利格巴納達時，她的父親John De La Roche35歲，母親Lady Maud Le Waleys Baroness Roche31歲。她於1275年與John Fitzthomas FITZGERALD 1st Earl of Kildare在愛爾蘭庫迪 斯利格巴納達結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1329年2月在愛爾蘭庫迪 奧法利艾登德利吉爾達享年74歲，埋葬在愛爾蘭庫迪 奧法利艾登德利吉爾達。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Blanche de la Roche于1255年出生在爱尔兰庫迪 斯利格巴納達时，她的父亲John De La Roche35岁，母亲Lady Maud Le Waleys Baroness Roche31岁。她于1275年与John Fitzthomas FITZGERALD 1st Earl of Kildare在爱尔兰庫迪 斯利格巴納達结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1329年2月在爱尔兰庫迪 奧法利艾登德利吉爾達享年74岁，埋葬在爱尔兰庫迪 奧法利艾登德利吉爾達。

nameMeanings.name: Roche
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Aidan, Declan, Eamonn, Fergus, Fionnuala, Kevin Patrick, Liam.
Irish (Munster) and English (of Norman origin): variant of   Roach  . Compare   Roch  .

French: topographic name for someone who lived by a rocky outcrop or crag, from Old French roche ‘rock’. In North America, this surname is also a shortened form of the variant Laroche 2. Compare Deroche .

variants.name: Laroche
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Fernand, Normand, Pierre, Jacques, Laurent, Adrien, Cecile, Germain, Marcel.
French:
habitational name from any of numerous places in various parts of France named Laroche or La Roche, from Old French roche ‘rock’, with feminine definite article la.

variants.name: Larocque
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Fernand, Henri, Marcel, Normand, Valmore, Yves, Achille, Amie, Donat, Emile.

French: habitational name from La Ro(c)que, the name of several places in various parts of France, from a regional variant of roche ‘rock’ (see Roche , compare Laroche ), with feminine definite article la. Compare Laroque .

Altered form of southern French Roquebrune or of its variant Rocquebrune: habitational name from Ro(c)quebrune, the name of several places in the southern part of France. The surname Roquebrune is rare in France, while the variant Rocquebrune is extinct there. Compare Larock , Lerocque , Rockburn , and Rocque 1.

variants.name: Rochet
meanings: French: diminutive of   Roche  , a topographic name meaning ‘rock’, or a habitational name from (Le) Rochet, the name of several places in various parts of France.
Catalan: of French origin (see above). This surname is also found in the Dominican Republic and Puerto Rico.

variants.name: Rochat
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Monique, Philippe, Pierre.
French: topographic name from a diminutive of Old French roche ‘rock’ (see Roche 2), or a habitational name from Le Rochat, the name of several places in the eastern part of France.

variants.name: Roach
meanings: English:

of Norman origin, from Old French and Middle English roche ‘rock, cliff, promontory’, sometimes a translation of Stone . The surname may have been topographic, denoting someone who lived on or by a prominent rock or rocky place, or a habitational name for someone who lived or came from a place so named, such as one of those called Roque or Roche(s) in Normandy and neighboring regions, or Roche in Cornwall, Roach Farm in Clyst Hydon (Devon), or Roch in Pembrokeshire (see Roch ).

in Lancashire perhaps referring to the River Roch, which runs through Rochdale; the river name is probably a back-formation from Recedham, an early name for Rochdale recorded in 1086, derived from Old English reced ‘building, house, hall’ + hām ‘village, homestead’. Alternatively, the Lancashire name could be a shortened form of the now rare or extinct surname Rochdale alias Rachedale, Rachdall, a habitational name from Rochdale (Lancashire). From the 18th century onward the name is difficult to distinguish from Roch , borne by Irish migrants to South Lancashire, especially Liverpool.

variants.name: Roquet
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alain, Camille.
French: topographic name from a diminutive of roche ‘rock’ (see Roche and Roque ), or a habitational name from (Le) Roquet, the name of several places in various parts of France.

variants.name: Rochez
meanings: Walloon and French: variant of   Roche   2 or   Rocher  .

nameMeanings.name: Blanche
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a nickname for a blonde, from blanche, feminine of Old French blanc ‘white’ (of Germanic origin). It came to be associated with the notion of whiteness as indicating purity, and was introduced into England as a given name by the Normans. A pale complexion combined with light hair was long an ideal of beauty in Europe (compare Modern English fair, which at first meant ‘beautiful’ and then, from the 16th century, ‘light in colouring’).

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: ‘Orchid’.

en.lifeSummary: When Blanche de la Roche was born in 1255, in Banada, County Sligo, Ireland, her father, John De La Roche, was 35 and her mother, Lady Maud Le Waleys Baroness Roche, was 31. She married John Fitzthomas FITZGERALD 1st Earl of Kildare in 1275, in Banada, County Sligo, Ireland. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. She died in February 1329, in Kildare, Edenderry, County Offaly, Ireland, at the age of 74, and was buried in Kildare, Edenderry, County Offaly, Ireland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Blanche de la Roche est née en 1255, à Banada, County Sligo, Irlande, son père, John De La Roche, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Lady Maud Le Waleys Baroness Roche, avait 31 ans. Elle a épousé John Fitzthomas FITZGERALD 1st Earl of Kildare en 1275, à Banada, County Sligo, Irlande. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée en février 1329, à Kildare, Edenderry, County Offaly, Irlande, à l’âge de 74 ans, et a été enterrée à Kildare, Edenderry, County Offaly, Irlande.

de.lifeSummary: Als Blanche de la Roche 1255 in Banada, County Sligo, Irland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John De La Roche 35 und ihre Mutter Lady Maud Le Waleys Baroness Roche 31. Sie hat 1275 in Banada, County Sligo, Irland John Fitzthomas FITZGERALD 1st Earl of Kildare geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb im Februar 1329 im Alter von 74 Jahren in Kildare, Edenderry, County Offaly, Irland und wurde in Kildare, Edenderry, County Offaly, Irland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Blanche de la Roche è nata il nel 1255 nel Banada, County Sligo, Irlanda, sua padre, John De La Roche, aveva 35 anni e sua madre, Lady Maud Le Waleys Baroness Roche, aveva 31 anni. Lei sposato John Fitzthomas FITZGERALD 1st Earl of Kildare nel 1275, nel Banada, County Sligo, Irlanda. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie. Morì nel febbraio 1329, nel Kildare, Edenderry, County Offaly, Irlanda, all'età di 74 anni, e fu sepolta a Kildare, Edenderry, County Offaly, Irlanda.

ja.lifeSummary: Blanche de la Rocheが1255年にアイルランドコウントイ スリゴバナダで生まれた時，父John De La Rocheは35歳，母Lady Maud Le Waleys Baroness Rocheは31歳であった。1275年にアイルランドコウントイ スリゴバナダでJohn Fitzthomas FITZGERALD 1st Earl of Kildareと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1329年2月にアイルランドコウントイ オファリーイーデンデリーキルダアで74歳で死去し，アイルランドコウントイ オファリーイーデンデリーキルダアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Blanche de la Roche(이)가 1255년 아일랜드 카운티 슬리고 바나다에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John De La Roche(은)는 35세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Maud Le Waleys Baroness Roche(은)는 31세였다. 그녀는 1275년 아일랜드 카운티 슬리고 바나다에서 John Fitzthomas FITZGERALD 1st Earl of Kildare(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1329년 2월 아일랜드 카운티 파리 에데더리 킬더르에서 74세의 나이로 사망했으며 아일랜드 카운티 파리 에데더리 킬더르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Blanche de la Roche nasceu em 1255, em Banada, County Sligo, Irlanda, seu pai, John De La Roche, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Lady Maud Le Waleys Baroness Roche, tinha 31 anos. Ela casou-se com John Fitzthomas FITZGERALD 1st Earl of Kildare em 1275, em Banada, County Sligo, Irlanda. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em fevereiro de 1329, em Kildare, Edenderry, County Offaly, Irlanda, com 74 anos, e foi sepultada em Kildare, Edenderry, County Offaly, Irlanda.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Blanche de la Roche родилась в 1255 г. в местности банада, коунти слиго, Ирландия, её отцу по имени John De La Roche было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Maud Le Waleys Baroness Roche, 31. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Fitzthomas FITZGERALD 1st Earl of Kildare в 1275 г. в местности банада, коунти слиго, Ирландия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла февраль 1329 г. в местности каилдар, эдендирри, коунти оффали, Ирландия в возрасте 74 лет и была похоронена в местности каилдар, эдендирри, коунти оффали, Ирландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Blanche de la Roche nació en 1255, en Banada, County Sligo, Irlanda, su papá, John De La Roche, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Lady Maud Le Waleys Baroness Roche, tenía 31 años. Se casó con John Fitzthomas FITZGERALD 1st Earl of Kildare en 1275, en Banada, County Sligo, Irlanda. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió en febrero de 1329, en Kildare, Edenderry, County Offaly, Irlanda, a la edad de 74 años, y fue sepultada en Kildare, Edenderry, County Offaly, Irlanda.
